page: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided locally via a 3200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present on this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng tests and uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth. Multi-core stress-ng execution, PassMark CPU tests (including encryption, physics, and extended instructions), and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the strong performance category. Redis SET operations also demonstrate strong throughput. Conversely, database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels. LLM inference speeds for a medium 7B model show average prompt processing but strong text generation throughput.

The CPU resources are allocated on a shared basis, which represents a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for memory-heavy workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU pinning. However, this shared allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-core loads. Given the high memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage, this configuration is appropriate for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, large-scale Redis deployments, and moderate database workloads.
description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 32 shared vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM. The hardware includes a 3200 GB local NVMe SSD and lacks a GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and uncached memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core processing and Redis throughput. However, database operations and static web serving remain in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-dense workloads, though it presents potential resource contention tradeoffs. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, and Redis deployments.

tagline: A memory-dense x86_64 server featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
